---
title: "Generate Volume 2 Chapter 2 graphs"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_notebook: default
---


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(scales)
library(latex2exp)
```

<!-- SUPPORT-ONLY:START -->
```{r notebook-utils, include=FALSE}
source("../../../R/notebook-utils.R")
```
<!-- SUPPORT-ONLY:END -->

```{r}
# Function to generate the plot
generate_plot <- function(materiality, std, cl, beta, deg, approach, dist) {
  x <- seq(-19.2, 19.2, by = 0.05)
  shift <- materiality / std

  # Create data frames for each scenario
  df <- data.frame(x = x * std,
                   y = dt(x, df = deg),
                   Scenario = "No misstatement")
  df_under <- data.frame(x = x * std,
                         y = dt(x, df = deg, ncp = -shift),
                         Scenario = "Understatement")
  df_over <- data.frame(x = x * std,
                        y = dt(x, df = deg, ncp = shift),
                        Scenario = "Overstatement")

  # Combine data frames based on the selected distribution
  if (dist == "Understatements") {
    df <- rbind(df, df_under)
  }
  if (dist == "Overstatements" | dist == "Both") {
    df <- rbind(df, df_over)
  }
  if (dist == "Both") {
    df <- rbind(df, df_under)
  }

  # Set factor levels for the desired order
  df$Scenario <- factor(df$Scenario, levels = c("Understatement", "No misstatement", "Overstatement"))

  # Determine bounds for "No misstatement"
  if (approach == "Negative") {
    upper_bound <- qt(1 - cl, df = deg, ncp = 0)
    lower_bound <- qt(cl, df = deg, ncp = 0)
  } else {
    upper_bound <- qt(1 - beta / 2, df = deg)
    lower_bound <- qt(beta / 2, df = deg)
  }

  # Calculate bounds for "Understatement"
  if (approach == "Negative") {
    under_lower_bound <- qt(cl, df = deg, ncp = -shift)
    under_upper_bound <- qt(1 - cl, df = deg, ncp = -shift)
  } else {
    under_lower_bound <- qt(beta / 2, df = deg, ncp = -shift)
    under_upper_bound <- qt(1 - beta / 2, df = deg, ncp = -shift)
  }

  # Calculate bounds for "Overstatement"
  if (approach == "Negative") {
    over_lower_bound <- qt(cl, df = deg, ncp = shift)
    over_upper_bound <- qt(1 - cl, df = deg, ncp = shift)
  } else {
    over_lower_bound <- qt(beta / 2, df = deg, ncp = shift)
    over_upper_bound <- qt(1 - beta / 2, df = deg, ncp = shift)
  }

  # Generate the plot
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y, linetype = Scenario)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Understatement" = "dashed",
                                     "No misstatement" = "solid",
                                     "Overstatement" = "dotted")) +
    xlab("Difference") +
    ylab("Density") +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")

  # Add annotations and segments for "No misstatement"
  p <- p +
    annotate("text", x = lower_bound * std, y = 0.06,
             label = TeX("$C_{.005}$"), size = 3) +
    annotate("text", x = upper_bound * std, y = 0.06,
             label = TeX("$C_{.995}$"), size = 3) +
    geom_segment(aes(x = lower_bound * std, y = 0.05,
                     xend = upper_bound * std, yend = 0.05),
                 size = 0.2, color = "black")

  # Add annotations and segments for "Understatement" if applicable
  if (dist %in% c("Understatements", "Both")) {
    p <- p +
      annotate("text", x = under_lower_bound * std, y = 0.035,
               label = TeX("$U_{.05}$"), size = 3) +
      annotate("text", x = under_upper_bound * std, y = 0.035,
               label = TeX("$U_{.95}$"), size = 3) +
      geom_segment(aes(x = under_lower_bound * std, y = 0.045,
                       xend = under_upper_bound * std, yend = 0.045),
                   size = 0.2, color = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
      annotate("text", x = -materiality, y = 0.01, label = TeX("$-PM$"), size = 3)
  }

  # Add annotations and segments for "Overstatement" if applicable
  if (dist %in% c("Overstatements", "Both")) {
    p <- p +
      annotate("text", x = over_lower_bound * std, y = 0.03,
               label = TeX("$O_{.05}$"), size = 3) +
      annotate("text", x = over_upper_bound * std, y = 0.03,
               label = TeX("$O_{.95}$"), size = 3) +
      coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2300000, 2300000)) +  # Beperk de x-as tot [-1,500,000; 1,500,000]
      geom_segment(aes(x = over_lower_bound * std, y = 0.04,
                       xend = over_upper_bound * std, yend = 0.04),
                   size = 0.2, color = "black", linetype = "dotted") +
      annotate("text", x = materiality, y = 0.01, label = TeX("$PM$"), size = 3) +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())  # Verwijder labels y-as
      
  }

  return(p)
}
```

```{r}
# Example usage with specific parameters
materiality <- 1000000
std <- 120000
cl <- 0.95
beta <- 0.01
deg <- 11
approach <- "Positive"
dist <- "Both"
```

```{r}
# Generate the plot
plot <- generate_plot(materiality, std, cl, beta, deg, approach, dist)
plot
```


```{r}
# Second example usage with specific parameters
materiality <- 1000000
std <- 240000
cl <- 0.95
beta <- 0.01
deg <- 11
approach <- "Positive"
dist <- "Both"
```

```{r}
# Generate the plot
plot2 <- generate_plot(materiality, std, cl, beta, deg, approach, dist)
plot2
```


<!-- SUPPORT-ONLY:START -->
```{r}
# Save the plot as a PDF
pdf_file_path <- save_book_figure(
  plot,
  "H6_Expected_diff_precise.pdf",
  width = 10,
  height = 6.18
)
```
<!-- SUPPORT-ONLY:END -->

```{r}


# Display the path where the PDF is saved
pdf_file_path
```


```{r}
generate_comparison_plot <- function(materiality, std_precise, std_less_precise, cl, beta, deg, approach) {
  x <- seq(-8, 8, by = 0.05)

  # Data voor het precieze model
  df_precise <- suppressWarnings(data.frame(
    x = x * std_precise,
    y = dt(x, df = deg) / std_precise,  # Normaliseer door std_precise
    Scenario = "Precise Model"
  ))

  # Data voor het minder precieze model
  df_less_precise <- suppressWarnings(data.frame(
    x = x * std_less_precise,
    y = dt(x, df = deg) / std_less_precise,  # Normaliseer door std_less_precise
    Scenario = "Less Precise Model"
  ))

  # Combineer de data
  df <- rbind(df_precise, df_less_precise)

  # Bepaal de grenzen voor het precieze model
  shift_precise <- materiality / std_precise
  if (approach == "Negative") {
    upper_bound_precise <- qt(1 - cl, df = deg, ncp = shift_precise)
    lower_bound_precise <- qt(cl, df = deg, ncp = -shift_precise)
  } else {
    upper_bound_precise <- qt(1 - beta / 2, df = deg)
    lower_bound_precise <- qt(beta / 2, df = deg)
  }

  # Bepaal de grenzen voor het minder precieze model
  shift_less_precise <- materiality / std_less_precise
  if (approach == "Negative") {
    upper_bound_less_precise <- qt(1 - cl, df = deg, ncp = shift_less_precise)
    lower_bound_less_precise <- qt(cl, df = deg, ncp = -shift_less_precise)
  } else {
    upper_bound_less_precise <- qt(1 - beta / 2, df = deg)
    lower_bound_less_precise <- qt(beta / 2, df = deg)
  }

  # Maak de plot
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y, linetype = Scenario, color = Scenario)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Precise Model" = "solid", "Less Precise Model" = "dotted")) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Precise Model" = "black", "Less Precise Model" = "black")) +
    xlab("Difference") +
    ylab("Density") +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
    theme(legend.position = "top") +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-1000000, 1000000)) +  # Beperk de x-as tot [-1,500,000; 1,500,000]
    theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())  # Verwijder labels y-as

  # Voeg annotaties toe voor het precieze model
  # p <- p +
  #   annotate("text", x = lower_bound_precise * std_precise, y = max(df_precise$y) * 0.5,
  #            label = TeX("$C_{.005}$ Precise"), size = 3) +
  #   annotate("text", x = upper_bound_precise * std_precise, y = max(df_precise$y) * 0.5,
  #            label = TeX("$C_{.995}$ Precise"), size = 3) +
  #   geom_segment(x = lower_bound_precise * std_precise, y = max(df_precise$y) * 0.55,
  #                xend = upper_bound_precise * std_precise, yend = max(df_precise$y) * 0.55,
  #                size = 0.2, color = "black")

  # Voeg annotaties toe voor het minder precieze model
  # p <- p +
  #   annotate("text", x = lower_bound_less_precise * std_less_precise, y = max(df_less_precise$y) * 0.1,
  #            label = TeX("$C_{.005}$ Less Precise"), size = 3) +
  #   annotate("text", x = upper_bound_less_precise * std_less_precise, y = max(df_less_precise$y) * 0.1,
  #            label = TeX("$C_{.995}$ Less Precise"), size = 3) +
  #   geom_segment(x = lower_bound_less_precise * std_less_precise, y = max(df_less_precise$y) * 0.15,
  #                xend = upper_bound_less_precise * std_less_precise, yend = max(df_less_precise$y) * 0.15,
  #                size = 0.2, color = "black")

  return(p)
}

# Parameters voor de plot
materiality <- 1000000
std_precise <- 200000  # Precieze standaarddeviatie
std_less_precise <- 350000  # Minder precieze standaarddeviatie
cl <- 0.95
beta <- 0.01
deg <- 11
approach <- "Positive"
dist <- "None"  # Niet relevant voor deze vergelijking

# Genereer de vergelijkingsplot
comparison_plot <- generate_comparison_plot(materiality, std_precise, std_less_precise, cl, beta, deg, approach)
```

<!-- SUPPORT-ONLY:START -->
```{r}
# Sla de plot op als PDF
save_book_figure(
  comparison_plot,
  "comparison_precise_vs_less_precise.pdf",
  width = 10,
  height = 6.18
)
```
<!-- SUPPORT-ONLY:END -->

```{r}
print(comparison_plot)
```
